title: K4: single-line content[].text summary across all 4 MCP tools
description: Replace the full `JSON.stringify(response)` payload in each tool's `content[].text` with a single-line human summary, while keeping `structuredContent` byte-for-byte unchanged. This eliminates the double-payload that triggered the 19KB payload warning (the agent reads structuredContent; content[].text is only a human glance). Applies to all 4 tools: fab_recall, fab_archive_scan, fab_propose (extract-knowledge.ts), fab_review.
action: In each of the 4 tool handlers, change the `content` array element from `{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(response) }` to `{ type: "text", text: <single-line summary> }` and KEEP `structuredContent: response` unchanged. Exact summary formats (count fields confirmed against output schemas): recall.ts:98 -> `Fabric recall: ${response.entries.length} entries (see structuredContent)`; archive-scan.ts:52 -> `Fabric archive scan: ${result.session_ids.length} sessions, ${result.dropped.length} dropped (see structuredContent)`; extract-knowledge.ts:72 (fab_propose) -> `Fabric propose: ${response.status ?? 'ok'} (see structuredContent)`; review.ts:77 -> `Fabric review: ${response.action} (see structuredContent)`. Note recall.ts:96 still needs `serialized`/`payloadBytesOut` for the ledger trace and payload guard — keep the `const serialized = JSON.stringify(response)` at line 88 for guard+telemetry, only the content[].text expression changes. Verify the summary field exists on each response type before referencing (e.g. review response carries `action`, propose carries `status`).

criteria: RED first: a test in mcp-server.test.ts (or per-tool *.test.ts) asserts `content[0].text` is a single-line summary and does NOT equal `JSON.stringify(structuredContent)` — this test fails before the impl change.

grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'JSON.stringify(response)' packages/server/src/tools/recall.ts` no longer matches at the content[].text position (line ~98); the only remaining JSON.stringify(response) in recall.ts is line ~88 `const serialized` used for payload guard + telemetry.

grep-verifiable: `grep -rn 'text: JSON.stringify' packages/server/src/tools/recall.ts packages/server/src/tools/archive-scan.ts packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts packages/server/src/tools/review.ts` returns ZERO matches.

grep-verifiable: `grep -n 'see structuredContent' packages/server/src/tools/recall.ts packages/server/src/tools/archive-scan.ts packages/server/src/tools/extract-knowledge.ts packages/server/src/tools/review.ts` returns exactly 4 matches (one per tool).

structuredContent unchanged: each handler still returns `structuredContent: response` (or `structuredContent: result` for archive-scan) — `grep -n 'structuredContent: response\|structuredContent: result' packages/server/src/tools/*.ts` returns 4 matches.
convergence.verification: cd /Users/wepie/Desktop/personal-projects/pcf && grep -rn 'text: JSON.stringify' packages/server/src/tools/{recall,archive-scan,extract-knowledge,review}.ts; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server exec vitest run
convergence.definition_of_done: All 4 MCP tools return a short human-readable one-liner in content[].text while the full data still rides in structuredContent unchanged, so agents no longer pay the double-payload that produced the 19KB warning.

files.change: Replace `text: JSON.stringify(response)` with single-line summary `Fabric recall: ${response.entries.length} entries (see structuredContent)`; keep `const serialized` (line 88) for guard/telemetry.
Replace `text: JSON.stringify(result)` with `Fabric archive scan: ${result.session_ids.length} sessions, ${result.dropped.length} dropped (see structuredContent)`.
Add RED test asserting content[0].text is a short summary and != JSON.stringify(structuredContent) for each tool.

implementation: RED: add/extend a test in mcp-server.test.ts that calls each tool and asserts content[0].text is a one-line summary AND content[0].text !== JSON.stringify(result.structuredContent). Confirm it fails.

GREEN recall.ts: keep line 88 `const serialized = JSON.stringify(response)` (needed for guard + payloadBytesOut), change line ~98 content[].text to `Fabric recall: ${response.entries.length} entries (see structuredContent)`.
GREEN archive-scan.ts: change line ~52 content[].text to `Fabric archive scan: ${result.session_ids.length} sessions, ${result.dropped.length} dropped (see structuredContent)`.
GREEN extract-knowledge.ts: change line ~72 content[].text to `Fabric propose: ${response.status ?? 'ok'} (see structuredContent)`.
GREEN review.ts: change line ~77 content[].text to `Fabric review: ${response.action} (see structuredContent)`.
REFACTOR: confirm each summary references a field that actually exists on its response type (entries/session_ids+dropped/status/action); fix tsc errors if a field name differs.

success_metrics: RED test failed before impl, green after
zero `text: JSON.stringify` in the 4 tool files

reference.pattern: structuredContent stays authoritative; content[].text is a human glance only

rationale.chosen_approach: One task spanning 4 files because it is a single behavior (C-003); splitting per-file would multiply agent spawns with no benefit.
rationale.tradeoffs: recall.ts keeps a JSON.stringify(response) at line 88 (for guard+telemetry) — that is intentional and must NOT be removed; only the content[].text expression changes.

risks: Removing recall.ts:88 `serialized` would break enforcePayloadLimit + the mcp_stdio_trace payloadBytesOut — keep it.
Referencing a non-existent response field (e.g. response.status on a type that lacks it) -> tsc error; verify field presence against the output schema.
